instructions.instruction: This skill has been merged into implementation-planner. Direct users to implementation-planner Mode 2 for all plan splitting and restructuring tasks.
instructions.relevant_when: A user asks to split a monolithic planning document, reorganize flat phase files, or restructure an existing plan into a hierarchy.
instructions.why_given: The splitter skill is now a redirect stub; all functionality lives in implementation-planner Mode 2 to avoid duplicated guidance.

instructions.instruction: Before splitting, detect the source type: flat .md phase files (manual workflow), JSON plan definition (automated via generate-structure.sh), or existing structure (validation only).
instructions.why_given: The correct approach depends on source type; applying the wrong method produces invalid output.

instructions.instruction: Apply the 3-7 items-per-group rule: flatten groups with fewer than 3 children, subdivide groups with more than 10 children.
instructions.relevant_when: Designing the hierarchy structure during a plan split.
instructions.why_given: Groups outside the 3-7 range are either too granular (noise) or too large (unusable); 3-7 is the navigation sweet spot.

instructions.instruction: Never delete source files before validate-structure.sh exits 0. Always validate the new hierarchy first, then remove the old flat files.
instructions.why_given: Deleting source before validation risks data loss if the new hierarchy is invalid.

instructions.instruction: Use descriptive, outcome-oriented directory names (e.g. 'step-1-extract-movement-logic'), never numeric-only names (e.g. 'step-1/') or generic names (e.g. 'step-1-stuff/').
instructions.relevant_when: Naming directories during a plan restructure.
instructions.why_given: Numeric-only and generic names force readers to open every file to navigate; descriptive names enable navigation without opening files.
